mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP and consumer credit growth indicate expanding aggregate demand, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0% signals labor market normalization, reducing recession risk premiums and compressing equity discount rates, leading to positive forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months where UNRATE drops below 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous low federal funds rates and rising consumer prices indicate accommodative monetary policy failing to contain inflation, triggering a stagflation risk premium that depresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive SPX returns occur in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates supply-side capacity utilization improving, which expands profit margins and drives short-term equity outperformance during manufacturing upcycles.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are flat or negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% over a rolling quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as construction and related sectors contribute to earnings.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with HOUST growth rates (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the federal funds rate signals an abrupt monetary tightening cycle, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months following a 0.10pp rate hike.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong nonfarm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor demand expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative when PAYEMS increases by more than 150k and UNRATE declines simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month growth in consumer credit, particularly when housing activity is stable, indicates a surge in consumer borrowing that precedes a short-term equity rally as spending expectations rise.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% and HOUST remains within 5% of its 3-month average.
